items.title: Ancient DNA reveals a chilling secret at China’s 4,000-year-old Shimao city
items.summary: Ancient DNA from China’s vast 4,000-year-old Shimao settlement is revealing how one of East Asia’s earliest complex societies was organized. Researchers found that its people were largely descended from local Yellow River farming communities, but also had genetic ties to distant southern and steppe

items.title: Going with the flow: Renewable energy battery breakthrough
items.summary: A 3D-printed battery designed at Queen's University Belfast could speed up energy storage research.

items.title: Scientists found mystery bacteria inside one of the ocean’s most elusive whales
items.summary: Scientists studying decades of pygmy sperm whale strandings have uncovered three previously unknown types of Helicobacter bacteria hiding in the animals’ stomachs. All four infected whales showed serious digestive damage, including inflammation, ulcers, scarring, and parasite infestations, although

items.title: A hidden “switch-off” signal could predict solar storms seven years early
items.summary: A newly discovered turning point in the Sun’s 11-year cycle could allow scientists to predict future solar activity years earlier than before. At this “switch-off” point, the most violent space weather abruptly ends, and the remaining number of sunspots offers clues about the next cycle’s strength.

items.title: The mysterious hum heard around the world may finally have an explanation
items.summary: The mysterious sound known as The Hum has been reported worldwide for decades, yet its source often remains impossible to find.
